title: MARA Sold 15,133 Bitcoins to Escape the Mining Trap - Now Comes the Hard Part
text: Marathon Digital (MARA) sold 15,133 bitcoins in March 2026 for $1.1 billion, using proceeds to repurchase $1 billion in debt at a discount. CEO Fred Thiel characterized the move as designed to strengthen the balance sheet and position the company for long-term growth while expanding operations beyond mining. The company faces post-halving margin compression, where per-bitcoin energy costs rose from $32,433 to $39,235 in Q3 2025. With Bitcoin down 21% year-to-date and MARA stock down 52% from October 2025 peaks, the traditional mining model has become increasingly unprofitable. MARA is partnering with Starwood Capital Group to develop digital infrastructure capacity. The deal targets 1 gigawatt of near-term capacity with potential expansion to 2.5+ gigawatts, positioning the company in AI and high-performance computing rather than pure bitcoin mining. The May 7 earnings report will test viability. Analysts expect quarterly losses between -$0.40 and -$0.51 per share with revenues near $183 million (down approximately 5% year-over-year). Success depends on converting infrastructure partnerships into signed tenant agreements before investor confidence erodes further. Institutional investors remain cautiously optimistic, though retail sentiment remains sparse and skeptical about the transition's credibility.
